candidates.why: Direct top-line sales/revenue line; matches total revenue values and is the most standard primary revenue label.
Also a valid total revenue line, identical to Sales here; useful as fallback if label changes by year.
This is a cost line, not revenue.
This is an expense/component line, not revenue.

variables.notes: Using revenue labels as a priority list; later code can take the first non-missing value.

candidates.why: Semantically exact COGS label, but preview is empty and underlying components are disclosed separately.
Best populated direct production/service cost component and appears to be the main cost-of-sales line.
Inventory change is only an adjustment/component, not full COGS by itself.
Explicit balancing row; should not be preferred over substantive line items.

variables.notes: Selected the main substantive direct-cost line rather than the empty exact-label placeholder or balancing rows.

candidates.why: Core operating overhead component.

Likely supplemental/disclosure style row; less primary than Share-based remuneration.
Appears as an empty supplemental label; less primary than the populated misspelled operating line.

candidates.why: Dedicated R&D expense line above operating result; best match despite misspelling.
Semantically correct but appears to be an empty supplemental label rather than the primary reported expense row.

'Owners of the Parent' is present on the Balance Sheet and is the preferred owner/parent equity measure because it excludes non-controlling interests, avoiding double counting with MIB. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Balance Sheet] Owners of the Parent
